free_text: Inelastic scattering of alpha-particles from 12C below
18.0 MeV
A 250 microg/cm2 self-supporting 12C foil.
The scattered alpha-particles were detected in a 700
microm silicon detector which subtended a solid angle
of 2.18 msr and had an angular acceptance of 3 degree
in the reaction plane. The detector was thick enough
to stop protons from the reaction 12C(alpha,p)15N,
which were separated from the alpha-particles of
interest. In order to detect the alpha-particles at
forward angles in the presence of recoil 12C nuclei,
calibrated nickel foil absorbers were inserted in
front of the detector. The target thickness was
obtained by comparing the inelastic scattering of the
alpha-particles from the 4.43 state in l2C with the
cross section of Morgan and Hobbie at 19 MeV, and is
uncertain to 10 %. This target thickness corresponds
to an energy loss of 100 keV for 12 MeV
alpha-particles.
Angular distribution was fitted with Legendre
polynomials and then integrated cs was obtained by
Sig = 4Pi * A0.
BP
Fig. 1, page 562.
The error bars include both the
statistical uncertainties and the errors arising from
subtraction of the three alpha continuum background
which begins at 7.37 MeV in 12C.
